twitter_thread: 9 out of 10 productivity apps I've tested can't replace paper notes, a $1,000/yr problem.
TCL NOTE A1 NXTPAPER solves this, but costs $400. Free alternative: Joplin open-source notes.
Using 5+ apps? Mistake. Stick to 2-3 like Todoist and Google Calendar.
Simplenote's minimalism helps me write 30% more.
A 'Someday' list can save 2 hours/week by avoiding main list clutter.
Saved $200/yr by switching from Evernote to free alternatives.
What's your most effective productivity hack? #productivityhacks #notetaking
pack.linkedin: In testing 20 productivity apps, I found that 90% couldn't replace paper notes, which can cost $1,000 per year. The TCL NOTE A1 NXTPAPER addresses this, but its $400 price tag is steep. A free alternative is Joplin, an open-source note-taking app. Integrating just 2-3 apps, like Todoist and Google Calendar, can also boost productivity. What methods do you use to stay organized and focused?
tiktok_hooks: Spent $1,000 on paper notes last year?
pack.reddit_headline: Can you really replace paper notes with an app?
